pairs.question: When did the 2007 Stanley Cup playoffs begin and end?
pairs.answer: The 2007 Stanley Cup playoffs began on April 11, 2007, and ended on June 6, 2007.

pairs.question: Which teams won the Stanley Cup in 2007, and in how many games?
pairs.answer: The Anaheim Ducks won the Stanley Cup in 2007 by defeating the Ottawa Senators in five games.

pairs.question: What was historically significant about the 2007 Stanley Cup playoffs regarding the previous year's finalists?
pairs.answer: For the first time in NHL history, neither of the two teams that played in the 2006 Stanley Cup Final (the Carolina Hurricanes and the Edmonton Oilers) qualified for the 2007 playoffs.

pairs.question: Which four former WHA teams missed the playoffs in 2007, and why was this significant?
pairs.answer: In 2007, all four former WHA teams (Carolina Hurricanes, Colorado Avalanche, Edmonton Oilers, and Arizona Coyotes) missed the playoffs. This was significant because it was the first time since 1994 that all four teams failed to qualify.

pairs.question: How many teams qualified for the 2007 Stanley Cup playoffs, and how were they divided?
pairs.answer: Sixteen teams qualified for the 2007 Stanley Cup playoffs, with eight teams from each conference.

pairs.question: Which team made the playoffs for the first and only time during their twelve years in Georgia?
pairs.answer: The Atlanta Thrashers qualified for the playoffs in 2007, which was their only postseason appearance during their twelve years in Georgia before relocating to Winnipeg in 2011.

pairs.question: Was there a year after 2007 when all three New York metropolitan area teams made the playoffs again?
pairs.answer: Yes, all three New York metropolitan area teams (New Jersey Devils, New York Islanders, and New York Rangers) made the playoffs together again in 2023.
